root_cause: Auth failures were caused by a network policy regression in version 2026.02.27.1 that blocked egress from auth-api pods in us-east-1c to the token introspection service on port 8443. A concurrent node rotation placed more auth pods into that zone, amplifying user-visible impact. The dependency itself remained healthy, so logs alone were misleadingly similar to a downstream timeout incident.

details: Auth API logs show timeout and incomplete mTLS handshake symptoms, but probes from other zones succeed
CNI agent logs record dropped egress packets for the auth namespace toward the introspection service in us-east-1c
auth_api.error_rate_us_east_1c_percent is far higher than fleet-wide error rate, proving zone concentration
identity_probe.success_rate_percent stays at 100 percent, eliminating a broad identity-provider outage
Commit b14c6d2 tightened auth namespace egress rules
Commit d8724a0 scheduled the us-east-1c node rotation that increased affected pod placement
Node rotation sets the context, policy rollout precedes impact, and rollback immediately resolves failures
The issue was intermittent at the user level because only requests landing on pods in the affected zone failed

recommended_actions: Require zone-scoped synthetic checks before promoting auth network policy changes
Validate egress policy targets against actual service identities and ports in every node pool
Alert on strong AZ skew in auth failures so partial-zone incidents are detected faster
expected_investigation_path: Inspect auth timeout symptoms and determine whether the dependency is broadly unhealthy
Break metrics down by availability zone and compare them with synthetic dependency probes
Review recent network policy and infrastructure changes affecting auth pod placement
Confirm the RCA against policy-drop logs and rollback recovery timing
